edge_hypothesis: kama_vortex_divergence exists as a template in signal_templates.py but has ZERO backtests ever run across 706+ outcomes. This is the FIRST-EVER test. The thesis: KAMA (Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average) responds to both trend direction AND volatility — it moves fast in trends and slow in ranges. When KAMA FLATTENS (slope near zero), the current trend is losing momentum. The Vortex Indicator (VTXP/VTXM crossover) detects the EMERGENCE of a new trend direction. Together: KAMA flattening + Vortex cross = EARLY REVERSAL DETECTION. The ATR gate ensures enough volatility for the reversal to be tradeable (not just noise). This is fundamentally DIFFERENT from our ACCEPT pool: all 24 ACCEPTs use either trend-following (Supertrend, MACD) or mean-reversion (RSI, ema_rsi_atr). kama_vortex_divergence detects TRANSITIONS between trends — a completely untested signal type. The template uses a normalized KAMA slope (divided by price) with a default threshold of 0.001. On ETH, this means KAMA must change less than 0.1% per bar to be considered 'flattening.' THREE OUTCOMES: (1) PF > 1.2 with 30+ trades: trend exhaustion detection WORKS — a new signal family for transitional regimes. This would be especially valuable since transitional PFs are rising (RSI slingshot 1.834, ea9p5k2m 2.765). (2) PF 0.8-1.2: the mechanism has some predictive power but KAMA slope threshold needs calibration — 0.001 may be too tight or too loose. (3) PF < 0.8 or < 10 trades: KAMA flattening + Vortex cross is either too rare or doesn't predict reversals. Need to check if the KAMA slope threshold is appropriate for crypto volatility. INVALIDATION: If all 3 variants produce < 10 trades, the KAMA slope threshold is too restrictive for crypto — need to widen to 0.002-0.005.

variants.name: kama_vortex_div_1h_8to1
variants.description: KAMA Vortex Divergence on ETH/1h with default parameters. First-ever backtest of this template. KAMA flattening (slope < 0.001 normalized) + Vortex crossover + ATR above-average gate. 8:1 R:R targeting full reversal moves. Expected: 20-60 trades — Vortex crosses are moderate-frequency events, KAMA flattening is the restrictive filter. The template is designed for trend exhaustion → reversal entries, making it a TRANSITIONAL regime strategy.
filters: KAMA flattening: KAMA_10_2_30 slope < 0.001 normalized (trend exhaustion)
Vortex crossover: VTXP_14 crosses VTXM_14 (new direction emerging)
ATR volatility gate: ATR_14 > 20-bar mean (enough volatility for reversal)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
Targeting full reversal moves from trend exhaustion.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: KAMA_10_2_30 slope flattening = trend exhaustion. First-ever KAMA test.
VTXP_14/VTXM_14 crossover = new direction emerging. First-ever Vortex test.
ATR_14 > 20-bar mean = enough volatility. 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP = 8:1 R:R.

variants.name: kama_vortex_div_4h_8to1
variants.description: KAMA Vortex Divergence on ETH/4h. Higher timeframe version — 4h KAMA flattening represents genuine multi-day trend exhaustion, not intraday noise. Vortex crosses on 4h are lower frequency but higher conviction. Expected: 10-30 trades. The 4h timeframe should favor this template because trend exhaustion is a HIGHER TIMEFRAME phenomenon — intraday KAMA fluctuations may produce false flattening signals on 1h.
filters: 4h timeframe: trend exhaustion is a higher-TF phenomenon
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: KAMA_10_2_30 flattening on 4h — genuine multi-day trend exhaustion.
VTXP_14/VTXM_14 crossover on 4h — high-conviction direction change.

variants.name: kama_vortex_div_1h_wide_slope_6to1
variants.description: KAMA Vortex Divergence on ETH/1h with WIDENED slope threshold (0.002 instead of 0.001). The CALIBRATION variant. If the base variant produces < 15 trades, the default 0.001 slope threshold is too tight for crypto volatility. Widening to 0.002 doubles the number of bars where KAMA is considered 'flattening.' 6:1 R:R (1.5x / 9.0x) captures moderate reversal moves. Expected: 30-80 trades. If this variant produces significantly more trades AND maintains PF > 1.0, the sweet spot is between 0.001-0.002. If PF drops below 0.8 with wider threshold: the additional signals at 0.002 are noise, not genuine exhaustion.
filters: KAMA flattening WIDENED: slope < 0.002 normalized (2x default threshold)
Calibration variant: does wider KAMA threshold capture more valid signals?
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 6:1. Needs 14.3% win rate to break even after fees.
Moderate TP targets shorter reversal moves from wider exhaustion definition.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: KAMA_10_2_30 flattening at 0.002 threshold — widened for more signals.
